summary: Matched the 112-byte fighter function ft_80081938 by replacing a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub with code that computes and stores two Vec3 damage-related fields from fighter collision ECB data, current position, and character attributes. The PR also corrected related struct and function signatures so surrounding code treats these fields as Vec3/float data rather than ints, and updated scratches entries for ft_80081938 and neighboring functions from percentage statuses to OK.
what_changed: Implemented ft_80081938 in src/melee/ft/ft_0819.c, replacing NOT_IMPLEMENTED with a matched body.

ft_80081938 now reads Fighter* fp from gobj->user_data, copies fp->coll_data.x4_vec into a local Vec3, reads left.x and right.x from fp->coll_data.xA4_ecbCurrCorrect, and writes computed values into fp->dmg.x193c and fp->dmg.x1930.
The computed dmg.x193c values are: x = current ECB left.x plus coll_data.x4_vec.x, y = current ECB right.x plus coll_data.x4_vec.x, and z = fp->cur_pos.y plus fp->co_attrs.x15C.
Converted Fighter dmg fields at fp+1930 and fp+193C from six separate int slots into two Vec3 fields named x1930 and x193c.
Changed ftCo_DatAttrs::x15C from int to float, consistent with its use in a floating-point y/z-position calculation.
Changed lb_80014770 prototype from bool lb_80014770(int, int) to bool lb_80014770(Vec3*, int).
Updated ftDrawCommon_800805C8 to pass &fighter->dmg.x1930 to lb_80014770 instead of passing fighter->dmg.x1930 by value.
Replaced stubs for ft_800819A8 and ft_80081A00 in the diff excerpt with decomp placeholder comments, leaving them still unmatched.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt to mark ft_80081938 scratch attempts as OK and also normalized several neighboring ft_0819 scratch entries from percent statuses to OK.
smart_moves: The match was achieved by improving type information rather than only forcing local code shape: converting adjacent scalar dmg fields into Vec3 made both ft_80081938 and the caller of lb_80014770 more semantically correct.
Changing lb_80014770 to accept Vec3* exposed the correct call pattern in ftDrawCommon_800805C8 and avoided treating a vector field as an integer argument.
Typing ftCo_DatAttrs::x15C as float aligns with the arithmetic fp->cur_pos.y + fp->co_attrs.x15C, which likely helped generate the intended floating-point instructions.

The implementation uses existing fighter collision data names such as coll_data.x4_vec and xA4_ecbCurrCorrect, grounding the match in known struct layout instead of introducing opaque temporaries everywhere.
Keeping explicit unused stack padding arrays in ft_80081938 suggests a stack-layout matching tactic for a small function where local variable placement mattered.
decomp_lessons: When a small fighter function refuses to match, check whether nearby struct fields are mis-typed; replacing scalar placeholder fields with Vec3 can resolve both the target function and downstream call signatures.
Function prototypes should be corrected based on actual use sites. A call that should consume a vector address should use Vec3* in the header and pass the address at call sites.
Character attribute fields involved in position offsets should be audited for float typing, especially when combined with Vec3 or current-position values.
Damage-related Fighter fields around fp+1930/fp+193C are vector-like in this slice, not independent integer slots.
Scratch-status updates can accompany a matching PR to mark accepted attempts as OK once the function lands.
naming_conventions: Unidentified fighter functions retain address-style names such as ft_80081938, ft_800819A8, and ft_80081A00.
Unidentified struct fields retain offset-style names such as x1930, x193c, x15C, x4_vec, and xA4_ecbCurrCorrect.
Typed struct aliases use project conventions such as Fighter, ftECB, ftCo_DatAttrs, HSD_GObj, and Vec3.
The diff preserves mixed-case offset suffixes already present in the project, for example x193c and x194C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced local variables f32 lx and rx for ECB left/right x values before assigning the Vec3 fields, likely matching load/order expectations.
Copied fp->coll_data.x4_vec into local Vec3 sp44 before using sp44.x, indicating local stack placement or copy behavior was relevant to the match.
Added explicit unused stack padding arrays of 36 and 56 bytes around the local Vec3, suggesting stack frame shaping was required for the 112-byte function.
Used a temporary ftECB* new_var pointing to fp->coll_data.xA4_ecbCurrCorrect before accessing left.x and right.x, which may have produced the desired addressing pattern.
Changed field and prototype types to Vec3/float to produce floating-point/vector-compatible code generation instead of integer operations.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document Fighter.dmg.x1930 and Fighter.dmg.x193c.
Find later matches for ft_800819A8 and ft_80081A00 in src/melee/ft/ft_0819.c.
Audit all lb_80014770 callers to confirm the Vec3* prototype remains correct.
Search for uses of ftCo_DatAttrs.x15C to identify its semantic name.
Search for additional PRs converting Fighter damage/collision fields from scalar placeholders to Vec3.
